source_quote: Symptom: White LED D11 absent; System Controller (LPC) unresponsive to SYSCTL UART commands. Likely cause: Always-on 3.3V regulator U14 failed or its output is shorted, starving the LPC11U24 (U18) of its supply rail LPC_VCC.

evidence.reasoning: Quote literally names U14; FUNCTIONAL CANDIDATE MAP U14 → U14.
Regulator failed = output dead/open, no LPC_VCC.

evidence.source_quote_substring: starving the LPC11U24 (U18) of its supply rail LPC_VCC
evidence.reasoning: Graph: LPC_VCC source_refdes=U14; if U14 fails, LPC_VCC dies.
U18 powered from LPC_VCC dies when rail dies.

source_quote: Always-on 3.3V regulator U14 failed or its output is shorted, starving the LPC11U24 (U18) of its supply rail LPC_VCC. Measure J22 pin 15 for 3.3 V; if absent, probe U14 output in diode-mode for short

evidence.source_quote_substring: U14 output in diode-mode for short
evidence.reasoning: U14 named explicitly with shorted mode; map U14→U14.
U14 is source for LPC_VCC; output shorted → rail at 0V.
